name: macos-verbs
description: Deterministic macOS actions for agents: app lifecycle, clipboard, files, notifications, appearance, volume, speech, screenshots. One self-contained binary with explicit permission tiers.

exit_codes.1: actionable failure (app not running, file missing, clipboard empty)

output.json_flag: --json on every verb; one JSON object per invocation on stdout with ok:true and an action discriminator; errors are text on stderr
output.human: single short line per action; lists are line-per-item

verbs.app launch <name>: launch by name, waits until running; confirmed:false in JSON if not seen within 5s
verbs.clipboard get: clipboard text; exit 1 if no text
verbs.open <path-or-url> [--with <app>]: open a path or any RFC-style URL scheme with the default or named app; prefix colon-containing filenames with ./
verbs.reveal <path>: select file in a Finder window
verbs.trash <path>: move to Trash (recoverable; returns trashed_to path)
verbs.window list [--app <name>]: windows with title, x/y/width/height, minimized; requires Accessibility permission (exit 1 with guidance if ungranted)
verbs.window move <app> <x> <y> [--index N]: move window to coordinates; requires Accessibility
verbs.window raise <app> [--index N]: raise window and focus app; requires Accessibility
verbs.darkmode get|on|off|toggle: system appearance; reports fresh-read state; first use prompts once for System Events automation
verbs.screenshot <path> [--main-display]: capture to file; requires Screen Recording permission for the calling app, fails exit 1 with guidance otherwise

safety.destructive_actions: trash only (recoverable); no rm-style deletion exists in the tool
